effort_complexity.rationale: A bounded but concurrency- and terminal-layout-sensitive replacement with explicit full-domain proof.

release_benefit: A SwornAgent operator can navigate every loaded release and slice inside a height-bounded TUI without unbounded startup discovery.

positive_scenarios: A 25-release project starts with ten records and exposes twenty after one explicit older request.
A short terminal reaches and visibly renders release 25 of 25 and slice 18 of 18 across a track boundary.
Right/Left and Enter/Esc navigate identical root focus transitions.
negative_scenarios: Excluded malformed older boards are not parsed before their window is requested.
A stale smaller-depth result cannot replace the desired larger snapshot.
A root scrolling test that stops before either final fixture row cannot satisfy AC-04.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: Bounded discovery plus complete root-level reachability evidence keeps the TUI fast and navigable as release history grows.

design_decisions.choice: Prime ten releases and grow depth by ten on lowercase o.
options: ten records with explicit lowercase o
design_decisions.human_decision: The repository owner ratified the existing ten-record explicit-loading contract through the replacement replan.
design_decisions.rationale: The interaction is bounded, predictable, and already represented by focus-specific help.

design_decisions.choice: Measure terminal chrome and use independent cursor-relative pane windows.
design_decisions.human_decision: The repository owner ratified measured height allocation through the replacement replan.
design_decisions.rationale: Measured allocation preserves selection-aware rendering across help and error states.

design_decisions.choice: Retain Enter/Esc and add Right/Left aliases through shared transitions.
design_decisions.human_decision: The repository owner ratified the existing alias contract through the replacement replan.
design_decisions.rationale: Spatial navigation improves discoverability without breaking existing keyboard behaviour.
